title: acorus calamus subsp. vulgaris
intro: Acorus calamus subsp. vulgaris, also known as the common sweet flag or sweet celandine, is a subspecies of the Acorus calamus plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Acoraceae family. The rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acorus calamus subsp. vulgaris include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Acorus calamus subsp. vulgaris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and is also used in aromatherapy and as a natural insect repellent.
